w:r.w:t: This assignment is designed for backend-focused MERN candidates interviewing with Waygood.
The product context is a study-abroad platform where students discover universities, compare programs, and track application progress with counselor support.
You are working on a platform that helps students shortlist destinations, evaluate tuition fit, and manage their study-abroad application journey.
The starter project already includes sample entities, seeded data, a React dashboard shell, and a backend code structure with intentional gaps.
1. Implement secure authentication with register, login, and protected profile APIs using JWT and password hashing.
2. Complete advanced university and program discovery with filtering, pagination, sorting, and a clean response format.
3. Build a recommendation engine using MongoDB aggregation based on student country, budget, field, intake, and IELTS preferences.
4. Implement the application workflow, duplicate prevention, valid status transitions, and status history tracking.
5. Add caching and performance improvements for key responses and document index choices.
6. Add tests for important API flows and document assumptions or architecture notes in the README.
Optional ideas include AI-assisted study planning, Docker setup, React dashboard improvements, and stronger security or rate limiting.
We will evaluate API design, MongoDB understanding, aggregation quality, performance thinking, maintainability, testing, and how well the solution reflects a real study-abroad workflow.
Share your repository, setup instructions, environment notes, and any assumptions needed to review your implementation.
